title: Rich Chewy Venezuelan Milk Custard
description: A creamy, lowcarb custard with a chewy texture inspired by Venezuelan tradition, made with milk, a healthy sweetener, and vanilla.
instructions: Grate butter into a 12‑cup muffin tin or use a silicone baking dish; set aside.
In a medium saucepan, combine milk, heavy cream, and water; heat until just below boiling, about 5 minutes.
Whisk in erythritol until dissolved; add vanilla extract.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg yolks until pale. Temper the yolks by gradually adding about 1 cup of the hot milk mixture, whisking constantly.
Pour the tempered yolk mixture back into the saucepan and stir continuously over low heat until the custard thickens and coats the back of a spoon (~170 °F/82 °C).
Strain the custard through a fine sieve into a bowl to remove any lumps or coagulated bits.
Divide the custard evenly into the butter‑greased cups or dish. Place the tin in a larger baking pan, fill the pan with hot water to reach halfway up the sides of the cups (water bath).
Bake for 35–40 minutes, or until the custard edges set but the center still has a slight wobble.
Remove from the oven, let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight to set fully.
Serve chilled, optionally dusted with a pinch of cinnamon or grated dark chocolate for extra flavor.
